title: AXS mechanism preregistration v3 draft — imprint-washout
registrationNote: Promotion of draftRevision 3 (design-draft) to a registered preregistration after the N7-6 branch-reachability verification confirmed the strong branch (both_supported) is structurally reachable under the v3.1 role-specific guard on critic families. preregId 'axs-mechanism' is invariant across v1->v3 (lineage). The filename retains the '_draft' suffix for path stability — every runner and the gate reference this exact path — and is NOT an indicator of status; the authoritative state is status=='registered'. derivedFromDraftHash is the canonical_hash of the immediately-preceding design-draft content (audit lineage). Evaluation families remain UNTOUCHED until the separate production go; registration does not itself run any evaluation family.
bindingNote: REGISTERED preregistration. The evidence gate accepts this prereg (status=='registered'). It is now immutable in spirit: amendments require a new version with supersedes + changeJustification per amendmentPolicy. Evaluation families are consumed only by the production runs that stamp against this registered prereg's hash.
supersedes: axs-mechanism v1 (registered, never consumed) and v2 design-draft (never registered)
revisionJustification: Draft revision 2 (pre-registration, procedurally free): the v3 utility guard is redesigned from RANDOM-parity to a role-specific guard following the branch-reachability analysis and advisor review.
A single rho=0.70 parity guard would exclude freeze_at_1 in 4/4 critic families, whose measured live-default coverage ratios are 0.38/0.69/0.59/0.59 (families 123/124/777/55555).

Coverage contraction under early freezing is the registered mechanism signature (imprint), not degenerate behavior, so a parity guard on the key manipulation arm would fail the program on the very phenomenon under study.

The dead-arm calibration artifact (configs/prereg/axs_dead_calibration_v1.json) grounds an absolute coverage floor empirically, between a provably dead constant policy and the minimum measured live key-arm coverage.

The advisor approved a role-specific guard: control and intermediate arms keep rho=0.70 live-default parity, while key manipulation arms carry a different guard (dead-calibrated absolute floor plus probe-responsiveness), which is not an exemption.
M2 is split into M2-IMP and M2-NOISE tracks under a strong-or-M1 binary: any guard failure demotes the affected track to unsupported (M1 only); no weak or caveated M2 band exists.

draftRevision 3 (integration alignment): the per-track canonical sentences (M-IMP, M-NOISE) are aligned to the registered scope-marker convention ('within the tested policy families' + 'in this controlled testbed') so that the gate-licensed sentences themselves satisfy the scanner's preregistered scope-marker requirement; meaning unchanged, only the scope prefix wording. Surfaced by the unpatched gate-to-scanner integration probe: with the previous singular-scope prefix, a licensed canonical sentence could never be published without a wrapper sentence.
changeJustification: The v1 pilot that motivated the v2 entry-point-ablation draft was invalidated by a config-drop harness bug: run_round passed per-call {"k": k} and AxsUcbPolicy/AxsYokedBonusPolicy.select resolved effective = config if config is not None else self.config, replacing constructor config; alpha/freeze/tie-break manipulations were therefore never applied; fixed in b7b64b42350bb424999569138b373b189c8704b1.

Post-fix re-pilot on 4 non-evaluation critic families (123, 124, 777, 55555) shows all v1 contrasts are behaviorally live after the fix; initial summary v2 was built from a collided-filename run (seedBatchId lacked base_seeds, so 12 reports overwrote to 4 files), falsely asserting 4/4 stability from 2 families' data — fixed in 89a8f4832cbca7be80acf0a1f1cb52b4a0855e33; complete re-pilot (pilot_v3, 16 distinct reports) is the evidence base.

The robust cross-family phenomena confirmed in the post-fix pilot are: early-freeze imprint amplification (delta_imp positive 4/4, large), trace-independent-noise destruction (delta_noise positive 4/4, yoked negative 4/4), tie-break invariance (4/4).
The alpha contrast is family-heterogeneous (3/4 positive, reversed in one family) and is therefore registered as exploratory-only with no claim license.
Strict monotone freeze ordering is violated in one critic family (family 124: quarter > freeze_at_1) and is deliberately NOT registered as a confirmatory rule.
v3 reframes the central hypothesis as imprint-washout; evaluation families remain untouched.

policyFamilies: AXS UCB variants (new IDs; C-011 frozen set untouched)

utilityGuard.appliesToNote: Every guard rule evaluates the cross-family MEAN of the arm-level utility.coordinate_coverage_mean over the experiment's families, never single-family values. Coverage is discrete in cells of 1/56 (~0.017857), so per-family values are coarse; the mean is the registered guard quantity.

randomReference.declaration: RANDOM is retained as a reference policy, but not used as the primary degeneracy guard for within-policy mechanism contrasts, because the mechanism tests evaluate perturbations of focused adaptive policies rather than coverage parity with random exploration.

controlIntermediateRule.rule: arm cross-family mean coordinate_coverage_mean >= rho * live_default arm cross-family mean, within the same experiment (mean-level parity; pilot critic ratios: quarter/half 0.73-1.05, alpha0 0.69-1.05)

keyManipulationRule.notAnExemption: Key manipulation arms carry a different guard, not an exemption: a dead-calibrated absolute coverage floor AND a probe-responsiveness check, both calibrated against a provably dead constant policy; rho-parity is replaced because the registered mechanism signature on these arms is itself a coverage change.

floorCalibration.granularityNote: Coverage is discrete in cells of 1/56 (~0.017857). Max dead-arm coverage = 9 cells (0.16071...), min measured live key-arm coverage = 10 cells (0.17857..., freeze_at_1, family 123); the floor is their midpoint. The guard applies to the cross-family MEAN (pilot freeze_at_1 4-family mean ~0.259, comfortably above the floor).

probeResponsiveness.structure: Every arm's report entry carries slateSequenceHashes: {family: {probe: hash}} where hash = canonical_hash of the ordered (round-order) list of selected cardId lists for that family x probe episode.
probeResponsiveness.requirement: For key_manipulation arms the gate requires >= 2 distinct hash values across the 7 probes within EVERY family.
probeResponsiveness.rationale: Per-probe traceHash distinctness is NOT valid non-constancy evidence because probe responses differ even under identical slates; the dead-arm calibration measured slate_excess_nmi exactly 0.0 in 4/4 families for a probe-constant policy.
probeResponsiveness.implementationNote: Runner emission of slateSequenceHashes and gate enforcement are implemented in the follow-up task; the contract is defined and frozen here, and the prereg leads.

freeze_at_1.note: Coverage contraction is the registered imprint signature, not degeneracy. Measured live-default coverage ratios in critic families 123/124/777/55555 are the reason rho=0.70 parity was rejected for key manipulation arms (it would exclude freeze_at_1 4/4).

axs_yoked_bonus.note: Yoked trace-independent bonus disperses selection; measured live-default coverage ratios 1.19-1.42 in the critic families.

utilityGuard.degenerateTripleObligation: must report degenerate, degenerateReason, includedInMechanismClaim for each arm

tieBreakCaveatMarker: subject to a tie-breaking sensitivity caveat (AXS-TB-001 soft_pass)

yokedSchedule.v3Note: reused by AXS-NOISE-001; derived from held-out pilot family 999; evaluation families untouched by its derivation

primaryContrast.name: delta_imp
primaryContrast.acceptance: sign positive in >=4/5 families AND cross_family_seeded_bootstrap ciLower > 0

AXS-IMP-001.strictMonotoneChainNote: NOT registered: violated in critic family 124 (quarter > freeze_at_1; see pilot summary v2); registering a rule already known to fail one critic family would be dishonest.

utilityGuard.rule: role-specific (see top-level utilityGuard): freeze_none / freeze_at_quarter / freeze_at_half -> rho=0.70 live-default parity; freeze_at_1 (key_manipulation) -> dead-calibrated absolute floor + probe-responsiveness check
utilityGuard.degenerateTripleObligation: must report degenerate, degenerateReason, includedInMechanismClaim for each arm

primaryContrast.name: delta_noise
primaryContrast.acceptance: sign positive in >=4/5 families AND cross_family_seeded_bootstrap ciLower > 0

secondaryContrast.yokedAbsence: axs_yoked_bonus sep not >0 in >=4/5 families OR ciLower <= 0

AXS-TB-001.target: delta_imp conclusion invariance over tie-break order

decision.strict_pass: sign unchanged AND within baseline 95% CI

AXS-ALPHA-EXP.m2ProhibitionNote: PERMANENT: this experiment can never contribute to or license any M2-track claim; it is reportable at M1/exploratory only.
AXS-ALPHA-EXP.note: Family-heterogeneous in critic pilot (reversed in 124); reported for the record, licenses no claim at any rung; ledger registration still required (no silent burial).

branches.name: integrity_fail
branches.licensedClaim: All claims blocked; gate check failure detected — no mechanism claim may be issued.

branches.name: both_supported
branches.condition: M2-IMP track licensed AND M2-NOISE track licensed (each track: primary contrast pass AND role-specific utility guard pass within that experiment AND AXS-TB-001 >= soft_pass)
branches.licensedClaim: Within the tested policy families in this controlled testbed, above-null slate separability is amplified when trace-conditioned policy state is frozen early in the horizon and is eliminated by trace-independent bonus randomization; continual trace-conditioned updates attenuate but do not eliminate it.

branches.name: imprint_only_supported
branches.condition: M2-IMP track licensed AND M2-NOISE track not licensed
branches.licensedClaim: Within the tested policy families in this controlled testbed, one-step trace imprinting amplifies above-null slate separability.

branches.name: noise_only_supported
branches.condition: M2-NOISE track licensed AND M2-IMP track not licensed
branches.licensedClaim: Within the tested policy families in this controlled testbed, schedule-yoked perturbation disrupts the structured separability pattern.

branches.name: no_claim_m1_only
branches.condition: neither track licensed: sign consistency <=3/5 families, ciLower <= 0, or ANY role-specific guard failure (control/intermediate live-default parity fail, key-arm absolute-floor fail, key-arm probe-responsiveness fail) demotes the affected track to unsupported — strong-or-M1 binary, no weak/caveated-M2 band
branches.licensedClaim: No claim at any rung above M1 within the tested policy families in this controlled testbed.

canonicalSentences.M-IMP: Within the tested policy families in this controlled testbed, one-step trace imprinting amplifies above-null slate separability.
canonicalSentences.M-NOISE: Within the tested policy families in this controlled testbed, schedule-yoked perturbation disrupts the structured separability pattern.
canonicalSentences.M2-COMBINED: Within the tested policy families in this controlled testbed, above-null slate separability is amplified when trace-conditioned policy state is frozen early in the horizon and is eliminated by trace-independent bonus randomization; continual trace-conditioned updates attenuate but do not eliminate it.
canonicalSentences.ALPHA-EXPLORATORY: Alpha effects were heterogeneous across critic families and are treated as exploratory.

canonicalSentencesNote: M-IMP / M-NOISE are the per-track canonical sentences (advisor verbatim). M2-COMBINED is the pre-existing v3 composite sentence, unaltered, licensed only by the both_supported branch. ALPHA-EXPLORATORY is M1/exploratory wording only — M2 use is permanently prohibited.

M2-IMP.requiresUtilityGuard: role-specific utility guard pass within AXS-IMP-001 (control/intermediate rho=0.70 live-default parity + freeze_at_1 absolute floor + freeze_at_1 probe-responsiveness)

M2-NOISE.requiresUtilityGuard: role-specific utility guard pass within AXS-NOISE-001 (axs_ucb_default rho=0.70 self-parity as live_default + axs_yoked_bonus absolute floor + axs_yoked_bonus probe-responsiveness)

M2.rule: the combined composite claim is licensed only when BOTH M2-IMP and M2-NOISE are licensed
M2.exploratoryExclusion: AXS-ALPHA-EXP licenses no claim and is permanently prohibited from any M2 track (m2Prohibited)

M3.requires: M2 (combined) + AXS-001 full alpha sweep + anchor validation

contrastLivenessEvidence.source: complete re-pilot (pilot_v3, 16 reports, 4 families x 4 experiments, runCommit 89a8f4832cbca7be80acf0a1f1cb52b4a0855e33) = pilot summary v2 rebuild
contrastLivenessEvidence.criterionNote: Every confirmatory contrast differentiates in all 4 critic families with paired deltas far exceeding measurement granularity.

residualChecksBeforeRegistration: H=16 spot check on one critic family preserves delta_imp and delta_noise signs (DONE — N5: signs preserved on family 124)

branch reachability under the v3.1 role-specific guard: confirm the strong branch (both_supported) is structurally reachable, NOT degenerate-locked as it was under RANDOM-parity (DONE — N7-4, see branchReachabilityVerification)

branchReachabilityVerification.verifiedUnder: v3.1 role-specific utility guard (absoluteFloor 0.16964285714285715, rho 0.70, probe-responsiveness >=2 distinct slateSequenceHashes per family)
branchReachabilityVerification.note: Re-run of the three confirmatory runners through the v3.1 runners (slateSequenceHashes + role-specific marking) and re-evaluation through the real gate pure functions (role_specific_guard, acceptance_recomputed_v3, tiebreak_invariance) on critic families. This reverses the N5 finding: under the prior RANDOM-parity guard freeze_at_1 was degenerate 4/4 and the strong branch was structurally unreachable; under the dead-calibrated absolute floor it clears (cross-family mean coverage 0.2589 >= floor 0.1696) and is probe-responsive in every family (distinct slate-hash counts 3/5/5/6), so registration is no longer a predetermined no-M2 program. Evaluation families remain untouched; this is a critic-family liveness probe, not the registered production result.
